codeReview.summary: The feature adds all requested analytics surfaces (conversion funnel, time-to-contact card, and age distribution chart) and integrates them into Analytics.tsx with recharts. However, the lead-age bucketing logic has a correctness bug that prevents the 60+d bucket from ever counting leads, which breaks VAL-EXPAND-008 behavior for stale leads.

issues.description: Lead age distribution computes `cutoff` as `now - bucket.max * ...` even when `bucket.max` is `Infinity` for the 60+d bucket; this produces `-Infinity`, and the condition `d <= cutoff` can never be true, so 60+d is always zero. This makes the age distribution incorrect and fails expected bucket behavior for stale leads.
Overall New→Converted percentage divides by `funnelData[0].count` without guarding for zero. If New count is 0, this renders `NaN%`/`Infinity%` instead of a safe fallback (e.g., 0%).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The react-frontend verification procedure (build + agent-browser manual checks) did not catch chart-data correctness defects in computed analytics values; heading/presence checks passed while a bucket math bug shipped.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification and transcript skeleton show UI presence checks on Analytics with real dataset (all New leads) but no deterministic data-driven validation of age-bucket math; bug remains in `src/components/Analytics.tsx:222-227`.

summary: Reviewed feature conversion-funnel-analytics at commit 9fa443f. VAL-EXPAND-006 and VAL-EXPAND-007 appear implemented, but VAL-EXPAND-008 is not reliably satisfied due to a blocking age-bucket calculation bug (60+d always zero).
